name: Pentagon No Plane Theory
description: The "Pentagon No Plane Theory" is a conspiracy theory asserting that American Airlines Flight 77 did not crash into the Pentagon on September 11, 2001. Proponents claim that the damage to the Pentagon was inconsistent with the impact of a Boeing 757, suggesting instead that the building was struck by a missile, drone, or that the event was staged as part of a larger government conspiracy. The theory gained traction in the years following 9/11 through online forums, documentaries, and books. Key evidence cited includes the relatively small size of the hole in the Pentagon's facade compared to the wingspan of a 757, the alleged lack of identifiable plane wreckage, and purported inconsistencies in eyewitness accounts. Mainstream investigations, including those by the 9/11 Commission and the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE), have thoroughly refuted the theory. These investigations provide extensive photographic and video evidence, structural analysis, and eyewitness testimonies confirming the plane crash. They explain the size of the hole by accounting for the plane's angle of impact, the collapse of the facade after the initial impact, and the internal damage caused by the plane penetrating several rings of the building. Debris from the plane, including engine parts and the black boxes, were recovered and identified. Eyewitness accounts, while sometimes varying in detail, overwhelmingly support the fact of a plane crash. The theory persists despite the overwhelming evidence to the contrary, fueled by distrust of government institutions and a desire to find alternative explanations for the traumatic events of 9/11.
subtitle: Claims 9/11 Pentagon attack was staged.

claims.claim: The damage to the Pentagon was too small for a Boeing 757.
claims.evidence: The initial hole in the Pentagon's facade appeared smaller than the wingspan of a 757.
claims.criticism: The initial hole was only the entry point. The plane penetrated several rings of the building, causing extensive internal damage. The facade collapsed shortly after impact, further altering the appearance of the damage. The angle of impact also affected the visible size of the hole.

claims.claim: There was a lack of identifiable plane wreckage at the scene.
claims.evidence: Eyewitnesses and early reports did not mention large pieces of plane wreckage.
claims.criticism: Significant wreckage was recovered, including engine parts, landing gear, and the black boxes. The intense fire following the impact would have destroyed much of the plane's exterior. Some eyewitness accounts do describe seeing airplane debris.

claims.claim: The attack was a staged event involving a missile or drone.
claims.evidence: Some claim to have heard explosions that sounded different from a plane crash, or to have seen evidence suggesting a missile.
claims.criticism: There is no credible evidence to support the claim of a missile or drone. Eyewitness accounts overwhelmingly describe a plane crashing into the building. The sounds described could be attributed to the impact and subsequent explosions and collapse.

keyFigures.name: Thierry Meyssan
keyFigures.role: French journalist and author who popularized the theory.

keyFigures.name: David Ray Griffin
keyFigures.role: Theologian and author who wrote extensively about 9/11 conspiracy theories, including the Pentagon attack.

keyFigures.name: David Dunbar
keyFigures.role: Co-author of 'Debunking 9/11 Myths: Why Conspiracy Theories Can't Stand Up to the Facts'

keyFigures.name: Kevin Ryan
keyFigures.role: Former whistleblower and proponent of the no-plane theory

timeline.event: American Airlines Flight 77 crashes into the Pentagon.
Thierry Meyssan publishes 'L'Effroyable Imposture' (9/11: The Big Lie), popularizing the no-plane theory.
The 9/11 Commission Report is released, concluding that Flight 77 crashed into the Pentagon.
The Pentagon No Plane Theory continues to circulate online and in alternative media.

sources.author: David Ray Griffin
sources.work: The New Pearl Harbor: Disturbing Questions About the Bush Administration and 9/11
sources.title: David Ray Griffin

sources.author: David Dunbar and Brad Reagan
sources.work: Debunking 9/11 Myths: Why Conspiracy Theories Can't Stand Up to the Facts
sources.title: David Dunbar and Brad Reagan
